title: Communicate in the Fear of the Lord
transcript:
Communicate in the Fear of the Lord.  
Ecclesiastes 12:9-13. Keep this in mind: The Teacher was considered wise and he taught the 
people everything he knew. He listened carefully to many proverbs, studying and classifying 
them. The Teacher sought to find just the right words to express truths clearly.

The words of the wise are like cattle prods —painful, but helpful. Their collected sayings are 
like a nail-studded stick with which a shepherd drives the sheep.

But, my child, let me give you some further advice: Be careful, for writing books is endless, and 
much study wears you out... 

We'd love to quote that when we're studying at school and we're having to face the exact final 
exams. No much study wears you out. Mom, do I have to do all my assignments? Yes, I did do. 
So we go back to the scriptures. 

That's the whole story. And here and now is my final conclusion: Fear God and obey his 
commands, for this is everyone's duty. 

So much insight here. You can take these passages and just unpack every phrase. So much 
truth. And when he talked about the books, the writing of books that is endless and much 
study wears you out, there's a lot of communication that doesn't actually strengthen you. It 
exhausts you. But God's communication is what builds you up. And our communication should 
always be marked by the fear of God. The fear of God does not mean that you are scared of 
him , it means the reverence of God, where you so treasure who he is and the things that he 
values - that to do things that are contrary to his will, that would bring grief to his heart seems 
totally unacceptable to you. 

In Proverbs 8:13, it describes the fear of the Lord. It defines it as the hatred of evil. It's not 
because evil is wrong and it hurts us. It is wrong and it does hurt us, but ultimately evil grieves 
God, because he designed us for good. He designed us to do good, to be blessed by good, to 
receive good from others, and anything that is not good - that destroys life -is evil. And, and if 
you fear the Lord, we hate those things. So our communication needs to be shaped by the fear 
of the Lord. And then, then we'll communicate as we should.
reflection_q1: Why should the fear of the Lord be fundamental in shaping our communication approach?

title: Be True to Your Words

And I think in my own life, this fourth one, keep the promises even when it hurts, is perhaps 
one of the most consequential. Sometimes you make your commitment, I'll see you tomorrow, 
I'll do this, I'll do that. And then circumstances change. And then it’s no longer convenient to

Will you follow up with your word, even when it's harder and it takes longer and it's more costly

title: Do Not Distort Your Neighbor’s Character
transcript:
Do Not Distort Your Neighbor's Character.  
Deuteronomy 5:20 says You must not testify falsely against your neighbor 

Lies destroy a person's reputation. They do damage to the relationships they have with others. 
When you lie against your neighbor, you diminish him or her in the eyes of their acquaintances. 
You create distrust and disrespect and rupture of relationship. Slander destroys the fabric of 
society. See, we were made by a loving God for love. We were made for relationships. We're 
given the qualities that are necessary, made in his image so that we can respond to love with 
love. We were made for relationships. Relationships thrive when truth is spoken. They suffer 
when falsehood becomes normative. If our private communication and our public media are 
filled with lies, if slander is normative, then relationships are damaged and society crumbles.

title: Do Not Listen to Ungodly Advice
transcript:
Do Not Listen to Ungodly Advice.  
Psalm 1:1-2. Oh, the joys of those who do not follow the advice of the wicked, or stand around 
with sinners, or join in with mockers. But they delight in the law of the Lord, meditating on it 
day and night.

In the key passage number 20, which you might have looked at already, which is about 
Proverbs 25:11 we are urged to listen to godly advice. Here we are warned against listening to 
ungodly advice, snarky scoffing, and mean hearted mockery is communication we should 
avoid. Avoid practicing it and avoid those who do practice it. Don't say it. Don't listen to it. Stay 
far away from it as you can.

title: Do Not Participate in Gossip
transcript:
Do Not Participate in Gossip.  
Proverbs 17:4. Wrongdoers eagerly listen to gossip; liars pay close attention to slander. 

If you have a problem with someone, if there's a something that needs to get sorted out in your 
relationship, go and talk directly to that person. Or if they're not available, talk to someone who 
can talk to that person who can make things right. Use your communication to repair the 
damage that has been done, to solve the problem, to restore relationship. Any other kind of 
communication, any talking behind the back, commenting to third parties who have no 
possibility of helping solve a problem only creates more relational trauma. It destroys relational 
harmon when you gossip. Gossip is saying anything about someone that you would not 
comfortably say face to face with them. Don't say it. And don't listen to it. Shut it down. If 
someone else starts talking to you evil about someone else, and they're not in the room, walk 
June 2023Page  of 2133

away. Distance yourself from communication which speaks ill of another when they are not 
present.

title: Do Not Participate in Arguments
transcript:
Do Not Participate in Arguments.  
2 Timothy 2:23-25 says this: Again I say, don't get involved in foolish, ignorant arguments that 
only start fights. A servant of the Lord must not quarrel but must be kind to everyone, be able 
to teach, and be patient with difficult people. Gently instruct those who oppose the truth. 
Perhaps God will change those people's hearts, and they will learn the truth. 

And similarly, Titus 3:2 says that God's people should avoid quarreling. And 1 Timothy 3:3 says 
that godly leaders must be gentle and not quarrelsome. The spirit of quarreling foments 
division. In contrast Proverbs 15:1 says a gentle answer, deflects anger, but harsh words make 
tempers flare. 

Both private and public communication should be done in such a way as to not throw fuel on 
the fires of arguments. Our words should facilitate harmony or they, they will incite division, 
anger, violence. What are you seeing on the media? What are you seeing in your own 
conversations? Are we doing communication in accordance to God's word?
reflection_q1: Why is healthy debate and dialogue critical for the development of a healthy society?
reflection_q2: What strategies can be employed to navigate contentious topics without resorting to arguments?
call_to_action: What will you do today to reflect the insights gained?

title: Do Not Participate in Complaining
transcript:
Do Not Participate in Complaining.  
Jude 1:16. These people are grumblers and complainers, living only to satisfy their desires. 
They brag loudly about themselves, and they flatter others to get what they want. 

Grumbling and complaining should not be a part of our lives or our communication. 
Ungratefulness always breeds relational discord. Gratefulness nurtures relational, harmony. 
Faith, hope and love, the three great virtues of 1 Corinthians 13 cannot grow in the soil of 
ungratefulness. When grumbling and complaining abound, those virtues die out. Grumblers are 
focused on themselves and what they don't have. They're the glass not only half empty but all 
empty; no matter what you give to them is never enough. It's a miserly way to live. Grumbling 
is the language of the selfishly entitled. A grumbler can never be satisfied no matter how 
generous you seek to be with him or her
reflection_q1: Why is it important for media content to focus on solutions and constructive criticism rather than dwelling on complaints?
reflection_q2: What impact does it have on society when it's citizens foster gratefulness?
call_to_action: How do you plan to implement today's lessons in your daily life?

title: Do Not Participate in Slander/Mockery
transcript:
Do Not Participate in Slander or Mockery.  
Nehemiah 4:1-4. Sanballat was very angry when he learned that we were rebuilding the wall. 
He flew into rage and mocked the Jews, saying in front of his friends and the Samarian army 
officers, “What does this bunch of poor, feeble Jews think they're doing? Do they think they 
can build a wall in a single day by just offering a few sacrifices? Do they actually think they can 
make something of stones from a rubbish heap — and charred ones at that? 

Tobiah the Ammonite, who was standing beside him, remarked, “That stone wall will collapse if 
even a fox walked along the top of it!” 

Then I prayed, “Hear us, our God, for we are being mocked. May their scoffing fall back on 
their own heads, and may they themselves become captives in a foreign land! 

Mockery seeks to diminish another in the eyes of society. It is demeaning, belittling, and 
casting aside, ignoring any good in their accomplishments. “That wall? A fox will tear it down.”  
This manner of communication and the heart that breeds it is never very useful in building a 
healthy society. These are things we should avoid. 

June 2023Page  of 2233
reflection_q1: Why is it important for public figures to lead by example in avoiding slander and mockery?
reflection_q2: What policies can be implemented by media organizations to safeguard against slanderous or mocking content?
call_to_action: What strategy will you develop from today's learning?

title: Do Not Participate in Prideful Boasting
transcript:
Do Not Participate in Prideful Boasting.  
Psalm 75:4-5. I warned the proud, Stop your boasting! I told the wicked, Don't raise your fists! 
Don't raise your fist in defiance at the heavens or speak with such arrogance. 

The proud person elevates himself or herself over their neighbors. This is the other side of 
mockery and slander. Mockery and slander puts other people down. Pride elevates yourself. It 
is inherently rebellious. You're not satisfied with this status quo. You're unhappy. You're trying 
to make the situation different by force of will and imposing yourself upon other people, 
dissatisfied with the status quo until you get to the top spot. We need to have a change of 
heart and a change of communication. Humility speaks graciously. Pride speaks boastfully. 

SECTION INTRODUCTION 
When we understand that we were created for relationships and that communication is the 
foundation for relationships, without communication, there is no relationship. Then it is

that Jesus the Nazarene was going by. So he began shouting “Jesus, son of David, have mercy

title: High Tech or High Touch?
category_title: Pursue Horizontal Communication with Your Neighbor
transcript:
High Tech or High Touch.  
2 John 1:12. I have much more to say to you, but I don't want to do it with paper and ink. For I 
hope to visit you soon and talk with you face to face. Then our joy will be complete. 

Wow! Paper and ink. That doesn't sound very high tech. Well it was in his day. It was the latest 
communication technology available to Paul. And he used it to its fullest extent. As did John 
and Peter and, and the others who wrote in the New Testament. We live in a time of incredible 
innovations in communication technology exploding, and we have more power in our phones, 
in our tablets, in our computers than any generation has ever had on earth before. You can 
speak to the whole world, But even though we have all this power in our hands, is our 
communication better? Technology alone does not communicate the message. It is the mode 
of communicating it, but some things you really need to do face to face. Some things warrant 
an in person touch. Sometimes technology is insufficient to say all that we want to say. You 
know, you can chat on zoom, but it's not the same. Try and give a hug or share a cup of coffee 
with a flat screen. It just doesn't work. 

Communication is essentially about relationships. And sometimes we just need to put our 
technology down. Sometimes I see a room full of people, all sitting side by side, each staring at 
their phones and have working their thumbs, talking to someone who's not there. Ignoring the 
people who are to their right, to the left or across the table. This shouldn't be. We should 
encounter one another in personal face to face dialogue and enjoy the benefits of the 
technology that we have in these days. But don't forget. It's about being face to face, looking a 
person in the eye and letting them know that they are valuable, that you love them. And you 
find joy when you're in their presence.

This is such a wisdom in this passage, he tries to find common ground with everyone. He 
knows you have to know your audience, if you're gonna be a good communicator. You have to 
identify with them. And though the message is the same, the way it's presented might vary 
depending on the crowd or the public that you addressing at that given moment. You tailor

Paul had one gospel message, but the way he communicated to the people with Jewish 
background or to Gentile background was appropriately modified for their situations. There 
was no one, right way. Be attentive of how you can be creative in taking the unchanging 
message and find the appropriate ways to communicate that in a given context. Always bear in

forget the “why”. The why is so that they might be able to spread the good news or share its

title: Know When to Be Silent

transcript:
Know When To Be Silent.  
Ecclesiastes 3:7. There's a time to be quiet and a time to speak. 

There are times when saying nothing is the best policy. Do not be driven by “I have to

Job’s friends show true friendship when they first came to see him in his distress and they sat

the tragedy he had suffered, they got together and traveled from their homes to comfort and

A number of years back, we were living in another continent. We had some dear friends who, 
the husband suffered, a major medical problem and had to be rushed in the hospital under 
intense surgery. Over the next week or two, my wife and I stayed with the wife, visited the

Friends, we don't always know why we get sick or why certain things happen to us. That's the

had just received a call and someone had, was blaming her for her husband's illness. And, 
trying to be helpful, I think, that person was in their own mind. But she came off the phone and 
she was crying and she was saying, came to and said, David, I just want to thank you all these 
people, trying to give them wisdom, trying to explain why my husband is sick, but you, you are

Because I've not tried to solve the problem. I was just there. My wife was just there and we

title: The Right Time and Place to Speak
category_title: Pursue Horizontal Communication with Your Neighbor
transcript:
The Right Time and Place to Speak.  
Esther 2:10 Esther had not told anyone of her nationality and family background, because 
Mordecai had directed for her not to do so. 

This is such an important lesson from this book. Because Esther listened to Mordecai and 
exercised self control over her communication, she didn't say everything she could have said 
about herself. She kept her ethnic identity a secret. It was unknown. And her appropriate 
silence enabled for this climatic moment to occur when there was the reveal of who she was in 
the presence of the king and the presence of the one who was trying to wipe out all of her 
people. And the silence at one point, and the speaking at the right time at the right place, 
turned the table on the dreaded enemy and resulted in her people being saved from a certain 
destruction. 

Listen to God, not only about what to say or how to say it, but when to say it, and he will guide 
you. Proverbs 15:23 says everyone enjoys a fitting reply. It is wonderful to say the right thing at 
the right time. 

CROSSOVER PASSAGES

connects with the other spheres. And some of the examples will be examples of positive

well together. We can learn from all of these situations.

Don't let your mouth make you sin. And don't defend yourself by telling the Temple messenger

June 2023Page  of 3233

51: Media/Communication and the Sphere of 
Education.  
Proverbs 2:1-2. My child, listen to what I say, and treasure my commands. Tune your ears to 
wisdom and concentrate on understanding. 

This attitude, the attitude of an attentive listener is central to the heart of education. The 
student should approach the experience of education with a heart dedicated to silence and 
submission. That's a strange phrase for us in our modern times. But this phrase, silence and 
submission, it was a common language in the ancient middle east, and implied to willingness 
to heed and obey instruction. It communicated that you are adopting a posture of being 
teachable. It's very hard to learn if you're not teachable. Your teacher can speak all they want, 
but if you're not participating with that right attitude has, that's just an essential part of 
communication that we listen well. 

Here’s some, some thoughts from a thesis that I wrote before. In Paul's time the Rabbis agreed 
that silence was an admirable attribute for the highest scholar. The command to learn in silence 
was not a derogatory comment, such as we regrettably might address to children, shut up and 
listen to what the teacher says. No, on the contrary as Simon, the son of Gabrielle, explained: 
all my days, I grew up among the sages and I found nothing better for a person than silence. 

This perspective of education was true, not only among the Jewish rabbis, but also among the 
Greek gentile philosophers. Plutarch said, but those who instantly interrupt with 
contradictions, neither hearing, nor being heard, but talking while others talk, behave in an 
unseemingly manner. Whereas the man who has the habit of listening with restraint and respect 
takes in and masters a useful discourse and more readily sees through, and detects a useless 
of false one, showing himself thus to be a lover of truth and not a lover of argumentation and 
contention. 

We need to listen well as students. And it's not just when you're in grade school and high 
school or in college. Throughout life, we need to maintain that attitude of a disciple. Listen, 
listen carefully to others. Anyone can teach you. Anyone, in any moment might have a truth 
that you need to hear. So if we adopt this attitude, communication and education work 
together to lead us into more truth.

Number 52: Media/Communication and Celebration.	33
June 2023Page  of 433

Communications/Media Sphere Introduction 
We now have the opportunity to look together at the sphere of media communication. 
Through media, God desires the transfer wisdom and the promotion of healthy relationships. 
Proverbs is full of pithy sayings, which emphasize the power of communication to do good or 
to do ill. There's great power, not only what is said, but in how it is said. Today with new media 
technologies emerging all the time, we have more capacity to communicate than ever before. 
But what are we communicating? How are we communicating? Is it bringing life or death? We 
should engage in media as followers of Jesus to advance the ways of the kingdom of God. 
Communicating always truth and love. 

SECTION INTRODUCTION 
This first section has to do with God and the Sphere of Media Communication. These six 
passages will highlight God as the most incredible Communicator in the universe. He is 
incomparable, and the way he communicates is a model for us and how we should 
communicate, whether that's in our personal relationships or in the societal sphere of media 
and communication. 

Number One: God's Ways Are Diverse.  
The passage we're looking at comes from 1 Chronicles 28:19 says this: Every part of this plan, 
David told Solomon, was given to me in writing from the hand of the Lord. 

God is a great communicator. And his methods of communication are many. Three times in the 
Original Testament we are told that God wrote. This was when he wrote the 10 
commandments. It was his own hand which inscribed those words on the stones. We’re told in 
Exodus 34:28 and, and Moses repeats it at least three times in the book of Deuteronomy. He 
didn't chisel those words into the stone. God's finger, God's hand wrote those himself. We also 
had this passage from David that God wrote the temple blueprint. It was God's words and he 
gave it to him in writing. Wow, that's amazing communication. He didn't just hear it from God, 
and then, and then record what he had heard. God communicated in written form. 

We also can remember when Daniel was in front of Belshazzar, the last king of Babylon and, he 
had been using the artifacts of the temple to worship idols, and God was not pleased with that. 
And God's hand appears in, in human form, starts writing on the wall and everyone was 
amazed and shocked and Belshazzar called Daniel in to come and explain that. So we have at 
least three instances in the Original Testament where God writes. And of course in John 8:6, 
when the woman caught an adultery is brought before Jesus, he too, stoops down and writes 
in the sand. What exactly we don't know, but we know that he does what his father has always 
done. 

Now, God is a great communicator. Hebrews 1:1 says that God spoke many times. And in 
many ways. And writing is just one of the ways that God's has communicated. If we go through 
the scriptures, the methodologies employed by God seem endless. He speaks through angels. 
That's pretty awesome. He speaks through a donkey. That's pretty astounding. He speaks 
through dreams and visions and prophets and signs. He uses a pillar of cloud in the day and a 
column of fire at night to communicate his intentions. He speaks through creation. We read 
about this in Romans one, that through what God has made, we can know about his attributes, 
his eternal power and his divine nature. And in Psalm 19 talks about how the heavens are 
telling of the glory of God. And he communicates through the things that he's made his, like 
the creation is his fingerprint. We can look at it and understand truths about who God is. And 
he speaks through a still, small voice. There's so many different ways that God has spoken. 
He's the communicator par excellance. 

June 2023Page  of 533

Now God speaks, or he commands or he promises, or he tells, there's a variety of synonyms, 
of verbs that communicate God's communication. And of all the things that God does that are 
recorded in the Bible, the thing that he most frequently does is he communicates. This is the 
top number one, verb action of God. And is the most repeated thing. Now, this is interesting 
because when people talk about, well, I heard God speak, or God spoke to me. People go like, 
oh, that's something really unusual. That's odd. Are you sure it was God, aren't you a little bit 
crazy? But actually in the Bible, when God isn't speaking, that's when it's odd. We read in 1 
Samuel 3:1 the author writes with astonishment ...now in those days, messages from the Lord 
were very rare and visions were quite uncommon. That was not normative. Normative is that 
God, this wonderful communicator, is always reaching out to us. It's not a problem about God 
speaking. The issue is, are we listening? 

Number 2: God's Ways Are Appropriate.  
Hebrew 1:1-2. Long ago God spoke many times in many ways to our ancestors through the 
prophets. And now in these final days, he has spoken to us through his Son. God promised 
everything to the Son as an inheritance, and through the Son, he created the universe. 

God's communication is always time appropriate. We read in Isaiah 28:10, how his instructions, 
his teaching is line up online. it says he tells us everything over and over. Like, don't you love 
it? Because he, he doesn’t tire of communicating with us. I often don't get it the first time I hear 
from God or the first time I read the scriptures, I need to be taught over and over again, but he 
says one line at a time, one line at a time, a little here and a little there. He gives to us, he 
communicates with us as we are able to receive it, as it's time appropriate, and we can handle 
and we can apply and understand. 

His appropriateness is not just doing the right ‘what’ the right content or the right time. But 
even the right, ‘how’, his means of communicating. We need to pay attention to the, how he 
speaks, not just the ‘what’ he speaks. This is the same message of God's love and God's 
redemption. God's desire to forgive and reconcile human beings to himself was the same 
message all through the Original Testament and the New Testament. It's what the prophets 
spoke of. It's what the law was purposed for. It's what the scripture's all about. But even 
though that message was there and constantly present, it didn't fully land, we didn't really get 
it, until God himself came in the person of Jesus, became incarnate and lived amongst us. And 
how he communicated, the fact that he became one of us, that ‘how’ that sacrifice, that 
incredible effort to make his message understood, not just that words about his love, but a 
demonstrable practical lived out love in our midst -that ‘how’ changes us because Jesus came. 

In these final days. He has spoken to us through his son. This ‘how’ is the means of our 
salvation. 

Number 3: God's Content Is True.  
1 Thessalonians 5:19-21, Paul writes Do not stifle the Holy Spirit. Do not scoff at prophecies, 
but test everything that is said. Hold onto what is good. 

The fact that God communicates truth is so very important. God's truth is constant and it's 
normal. We live in an age where skepticism is rampant, where everyone doubts the existence 
of objective truth. And everyone seeks their own truth. Well, whatever you feel or I feel or you 
think, or I think doesn't make - our feelings and our thoughts don't make that thing true or not 
June 2023Page  of 633

true. It's true apart from our existence, it's true because it matches reality. And God's truth, 
God's communication is always true. 

Let me just reflect some passages with you. Proverbs 30:5: Every word of God proves true... 
His commandments are right. They, when we live according to them, they prove true. Life is 
better that way. His promises are true when we trust him and he, he fulfills them. We can see 
he's trustworthy. His every word proves true. 

Isaiah 45:19 God is speaking. And he says: ... I, the Lord speak only what is true and declare 
only what is right. Are you looking for what is true and right in your life? Look to God. He's the 
only one who can guarantee that always its true, always its right. And when we think about this 
sphere, we need to model our actions after this action of God. 

Jesus, when he was before Pilate on the, at the time of just before his crucifixion said this in 
John 18:37, ...actually, I was born and came into this world to testify to the truth. All who love 
the truth recognized that what I say is true. 

When I was in university, I was in a dorm that was, you had applied to get into was kind of the, 
the college nerds, from the different fields they got together. Our dorm was different. This was 
secular university. Other dorms on weekends would have keggers and parties with beer and

way I pitched it was this letter is foundational to Western civilization. So let's read about it.

So I'm having a Bible study, but it's not a Bible study, it’s a literature study because you know, 
95% of the people there are non-believers. And so I remember one day, because in 1 John it 
talks a lot about truth, one of the most brilliant guys on campus who was studying philosophy

if all these comments that John is making about truth? What if they are really are objectively 
true? And this is how he answered. He said, well, I've dedicated my life to the search of truth. 
So I don't want to know if this is really true or not. Because if I find out that it's true, then my

What? Are you kidding me? The world plays so many games with truth. They speak about 
truth, but avoid it when it brings any implications or consequences on their life. We, as 
followers of Jesus need to know there's a constant truth and we have to discover it as we 
listen to God, as we walk in his ways, we need to be a people who are consequential in 
applying that truth to our life. We don't try to shape the Bible to match our world. We need to 
shape our world to match what the Bible says. 

Finally, in the these passages you can see and Acts 14:3, Paul and Barnabas on their first 
missionary journey. They say, ...and the Lord proved their message was true by giving them 
power to do miraculous signs and wonders. And 1 Thessalonians 1:5  Paul writes: for when we 
brought you the Good News, it was not only with words, but also with power, for the Holy Spirit 
gave you full assurance that what we said was true. God’s mighty acts are confirmations. 
Those things that are absolutely impossible for the paralyzed to walk, for the dead to rise, for 
the blind to see —these are confirming signs that this is indeed the true message. 

So finally, 2 Timothy 3:14-16. Paul writes to his protege, Timothy: but you must remember...
remain faithful to the things you've been taught. You know they are true, for you know you can 
trust those who taught you. So we can know truth because it, it matches reality. You know, it's 
June 2023Page  of 733

true because it's supported by the miraculous confirmation of God. We can know it's true 
because we can see the lives of the people who've passed us on to us. You have been taught 
the holy Scriptures from childhood, and they have given you the wisdom to receive the 
salvation that comes by trusting in Christ Jesus. All scripture is inspired by God and is useful to 
teach us what is true and to make us realize what is wrong in our lives. It corrects us when we 
are wrong and teaches us to do what is right. 

Number 4: God's Content Is Trustworthy.  
Luke 1:1-4. at the outset of his gospel Luke writes these words, talking about the methodology 
he employed in order to make sure that what he was writing about we are reading is absolutely 
reliable. Many people he says, have set out to write accounts about the events that have been 
fulfilled among us. They used the eyewitness report circulating among us from the early 
disciples. Having carefully investigated everything from the beginning. I also have decided to 
write in an accurate account for you, most honorable Theopholius, so you can be certain- 
certain of the truth of everything you were taught. 

You can count on it. You can rely on it. You can build your life on it. This content is trustworthy. 
In our world there are many substantial claims that we hear about all the time. You just have to 
go online or turn on the television or listen to the radio. And, and you know, if you use this 
toothpaste you become the most popular person in your class. Those are unsubstantiated 
claims, but not so with God's word. This is not a marketing device. It's not something that's 
been invented in the boardroom, where people are trying to pitch their own products. This is 
God's word. It's reliable. It's trustworthy. You can go to the bank with it. It's absolutely true. 

Number 5: God's Communication Is Transformative.  
Colossians 1:6 says this, This same Good News that came to you is going out all over the 
world. It is bearing fruit everywhere by changing lives, just as it changed your lives from the day 
you first heard and understood the truth about God's wonderful grace. 

God's truth is applicable. It changes lives. It was designed to reshape us, to take us wherever 
we are in our life, with whatever degree of brokenness we have and to make us into who we 
are always intended to be. Sons and daughters of the Living God. Jesus says this in John 
8:31-32 ...you are truly my disciples if you remain faithful to my teachings. And you will know 
the truth, and the truth will set you free. 

Allowing God's word to shape your life, to embrace this truth, is a freeing activity. It sets us free 
from the distortions and confusions and the habits and the addictions and the, and the issues 
that keep us bound and destroy our relationships. God's word brings transformation. 

Number 6: God's Communication Gives Life.  
John 20:30-31. The disciples saw Jesus do many other miraculous signs in addition to the 
ones recorded in this book. But these are written so that you may continue to believe that 
Jesus is the Messiah, the Son of God, and that by believing in him you will have life by the 
power of his name. 

This message, this communication, this truth of God is, is what gives us life. When we speak of 
life here, it's more than mere existence. It's more than your heart beating, and your lungs 
sucking in oxygen. It's about the fullness of life. The full realization of the potential. In John 
10:10, Jesus says my purpose is to give them a rich and satisfying life or in some translations,  
have them experience life more abundantly. We all have some measure of life, but we don't 
have it to its full. We have not realized the fullest expression imaginable. And Jesus comes to 
June 2023Page  of 833

take our life, which sometimes feels very far away from the dreams we've dreamed, the hopes 
we've yearned after. And he says, I can come and bring a true change. I can give you life that is 
complete, full, meaningful. That is sustainable. And it gives you value and dignity and worth. 

God's words are designed to do us good. You see, God is a life-Giver, He’s a life-Redeemer. 
He's a life-sustainer. He's a life-Enhancer. God is all about doing good in your life and my life. 
And if we’re to communicate like God communicates, then our communication must also give 
life, and enhance life, restore life. That's what God's purposes are for this sphere. 

SECTION INTRODUCTION 
In this next section, we're gonna look at God's purposes of communication. Why this sphere is 
so very important to our life as individuals and to our societies, to our cultures. There are very 
clear reasons why God has designed communication. You see, he created the world, made us 
in his image. He's the great communicator. He’s given us the capacity to communicate 
because he has that. Because communication is the foundation for relationship. Without 
communication you can't know one another, you can't love one another, you can't trust one 
another. So communication is foundational to the very essence of who we are as human 
beings. Beings made in the image of God, beings capable of receiving and giving love, 
responding to love- both vertically with God and horizontally with one another. Without 
communication, we can't be human. We can't be who we're intended to be. So understanding 
this is so essential. When this gets distorted in our society, human existence suffers; when this 
is done well, then human existence thrives. 

Number 7: Creates Unity Around God's Purpose.  
That's what communication is designed to do Zephaniah 3:9. Then I will purify the speech of all 
people, so that everyone can worship the Lord together. 

Our speech needs purification. Our speech is often not as it should be. It needs to be 
corrected. It needs to be adjusted. It needs to be transformed. And when it is, it allows us to 
come together. It allows for unity to happen. It creates communion and fellowship, and 
friendship. When our speech is off, relationships suffer. When our speech is on track, our 
relationships are as they should be. Contemporary media often thrives around discord. When 
there is a crisis or a problem or a conflict. Wow. The ratings go up. Everyone who wants to 
watch this thing play out and see the fight, fight, fight, fight, come. And, and they look excitedly 
at that, but that's not the way of God. Communication should not be about highlighting 
discord, but facilitating unity. This does not mean to cover up issues, but to really look at 
issues in depth so that we can address what is wrong and, and find the way to come together. 
The goal is creating a sense of common identity, of common purpose or mission in life. A 
profound harmony that supersedes cultural divisions. 

Paul writes in Romans why he wrote that letter, it wasn't primarily just to do an exposition of his 
theology. He had a pastoral purpose. He wanted to bring about change. You see, in the church, 
there were believers who were of Jewish background and others who were of Gentile 
background. And because of numerous circumstances, they were not worshiping together. 
They were each in their own separate silos. And he writes in order to help them understand 
they both have the same problem with sin. They both have the same solution to that problem, 
grace, through the made possible through Jesus sacrificial life and death on the cross of 
resurrection. And both are to come together. And so he gives the theology he gives the 
practice, then he sums up at, toward the very end of his letter this is his heart, May God who 
gives this patience and encouragement, help you live in complete harmony with each other. 

June 2023Page  of 933

That's why he writes. He wants the Jewish background believers, and Gentile background 
believers to live in complete harmony with each other as is fitting for followers of Christ Jesus. 
Then all of you can join together with one voice, giving praise and glory to God, the father of 
our Lord Jesus Christ. He is doing here exactly what Zephaniah had prophesied. He's building 
his whole theology and practice around helping them come together as one to honor God. 

lf we're communicating well, if we're communicating as we should, it should be strengthening 
unity across our societies, across our world. And having people come together, not in splitting 
them apart. When your communication, when your media is augmenting divisions, you're not 
using the media as it should. When it is helping people understand where there have been 
problems so they can address them and come together, then God's purposes are being 
fulfilled. 

Number 8: Creates Life-Enhancing Situations.  
Proverbs 10:11. The words of the godly are a life giving fountain. The words of the wicked 
conceal violent intentions. 

As I was getting ready to film this set, I went to the barbershop yesterday, cut my hair. And I 
walked in and I had a, a lady come and take me through the chair to cut my hair. And she was 
maybe in little late forties, early fifties. And I asked, how are you doing? She kind of grunted 
said, are you doing okay? And she, oh, it's just the work. I said, having a bad day? Oh, you 
know, it's just the work. And you could tell she was, she was doing what she was doing, you 
know, because she had to get the salary and pay the bills and take care of her family and do 
the things that people do. And so many people do the work that it’s because they have to, 
there's not finding joy fulfillment in it. 

So I sat there and she started cutting my hair, and I struck a up a conversation with her. I said, I 
said, you know, you do something really important. I said, you know, when people come in 
here or they come in because they don't like the way they look. Their, their hair doesn't look 
good. And, and by the time you finished in the half hour, you show them the mirror at the end 
and go - Wow, I look good. I'm looking great. And you, you, for what you do, you give them a 
sense of renew sense, worth of value of, of affirmation. And she looked at me said, wow, you 
know, I haven't thought of that. And I just held up a mirror to her. She would hold up a mirror 
for me to see how my hair was cut. I held up a mirror to her so you could see her, her life was 
touching people. 

She'd been doing this for about 30 years. She said asking, do you know how many people you 
would've touched in those times? The thousands of not thousands. And her eyes grew big. 
And my communication just enabled her to see that her life mattered. But she was doing more 
than just getting a paycheck. She was more than just meeting the needs of her family. She was 
doing good in her community and her society. 

God's words enhance life. And we can do this anywhere, everywhere. It doesn't have to be 
when you just give a religious message, you can just speak truth and affirmation. Proverbs

and Peter and, and the others who wrote in the New Testament. We live in a time of incredible 
innovations in communication technology exploding, and we have more power in our phones, 
in our tablets, in our computers than any generation has ever had on earth before. You can 
speak to the whole world, But even though we have all this power in our hands, is our 
communication better? Technology alone does not communicate the message. It is the mode 
of communicating it, but some things you really need to do face to face. Some things warrant 
an in person touch. Sometimes technology is insufficient to say all that we want to say. You 
know, you can chat on zoom, but it's not the same. Try and give a hug or share a cup of coffee 
with a flat screen. It just doesn't work. 

Communication is essentially about relationships. And sometimes we just need to put our 
technology down. Sometimes I see a room full of people, all sitting side by side, each staring at 
their phones and have working their thumbs, talking to someone who's not there. Ignoring the 
people who are to their right, to the left or across the table. This shouldn't be. We should 
encounter one another in personal face to face dialogue and enjoy the benefits of the 
technology that we have in these days. But don't forget. It's about being face to face, looking a 
person in the eye and letting them know that they are valuable, that you love them. And you 
find joy when you're in their presence. 

Number 42: In Times of Crisis and Danger.  
Ezekiel 3:16-21 After seven days the Lord gave me a message. He said, Son of man, I have 
appointed you as a watchman for Israel. Whenever you receive a message from me, warn 
people immediately. If I warn the wicked saying, you are under the penalty of death, but you fail 
to deliver the warning, they will die in their sins and I will hold you responsible for their deaths. 
If you warn them and they refuse to be repent and keep on sinning, they will die in their sins. 
But you'll have saved yourselves because you have obeyed me.

If righteous people turn away from their righteous behavior and ignore the obstacles I put in 
their way, they will die. And if you do not warn them, they will die in their sins. None of their 
righteous acts will be remembered, and I will hold you responsible for their deaths. But if you 
warn righteous people not to sin and they listen to you and do not sin, they will live, and you 
will have saved yourself too 

Clear communication is essential in times of crisis. And God raises up his people in the sphere 
of media and other spheres to proclaim truth in times of need, in times of crisis. We need to 
have this perspective of a watchman. The watchman sits on a high tower at a vantage point on 
June 2023Page  of 2833

the wall of the city. So they have a large panoramic view of all the area around the city, can 
easily see anyone approaching any danger, anything that might threaten the city. We need to 
have that broad perspective of the world, station yourself to have the biggest picture possible. 
We live in a day when social media and, and all of the algorithms get us narrower and narrower, 
narrower. You like something five times and that's all that ever appears on your devices. Break 
out of the narrowness of the molds that our digital world tries to force us into and have a broad 
perspective. 

And in the midst of that, be attentive to the voice of God. When he says in this situation speak 
thus, in that situation, speak this other thing, then that is really important- to hear and obey, 
says here immediately. Public communication can be a matter of life or death. Embrace the role 
of communicating responsibly. The common good depends on how clearly and quickly we 
communicate. It needs to be accurate. It needs to be timely when we're in crisis. Your role as a 
communicator is so vital. Have a big picture. Listen to God, obey. Communicate 

Number 43: In times of joy and good news.  
This comes from Acts 19:17-20. The story of what happened spread quickly all through 
Ephesus, to Jews and Greeks alike. A solemn fear descended on the city, and the name of the 
Lord, Jesus was greatly honored. Many who became believers, confessed their sinful 
practices. A number of them who had been practicing sorcery brought their incantation books 
and burned them at a public bonfire. The value that books was several million dollars. So the 
message about the Lord spread widely and had a powerful effect. 

Paul was in Ephesus on his missionary journey. And just a few verses earlier, it says that he had 
done extraordinary miracles, not just miracles, but extraordinary. What it meant is that people 
were giving to him handkerchiefs and he was touching them. And the handkerchiefs would be

several other stories that lead up to this passage. This was a time of just the goodness of God 
being displayed as healing and miracles were going on. Communication was so important in 
the midst of that situation, the story of what God was doing and needed to spread throughout 
the province of Asia (which today would be Western Turkey for us) so that people would know 
what God was doing. We need to be good communicators. Not only when there are hard, 
difficult times, but in times when there's joy and good news.

Number 44: Communicating In Ways Appropriate for 
Your Hearers.  
1 Corinthians 9:19-23. Even though I am a free man with no master, Paul writes, I have become 
a slave to all people to bring many to Christ. When I was with the Jews, I lived like a Jew to 
bring the Jews to Christ. When I was with those who follow the Jewish law, I too, lived under 
that law, even though I'm not subject to the law, I did this so I could bring to, to Christ those 
who are under the law. When I am with the Gentiles who do not follow the Jewish law, I too, 
live apart from that law so I can bring them to Christ, but I do not ignore the law of God. I obey 
the law of Christ. When I'm with those who weak, I share their weakness for I want to bring 
their, the weak to Christ. Yes. I try to find common ground with everyone doing everything I can 
to save some. I do everything to spread the good news and share in its blessings. 

This is such a wisdom in this passage, he tries to find common ground with everyone. He 
knows you have to know your audience, if you're gonna be a good communicator. You have to 
identify with them. And though the message is the same, the way it's presented might vary 
depending on the crowd or the public that you addressing at that given moment. You tailor 
your communication to meet their needs. 

June 2023Page  of 2933

Paul had one gospel message, but the way he communicated to the people with Jewish 
background or to Gentile background was appropriately modified for their situations. There 
was no one, right way. Be attentive of how you can be creative in taking the unchanging 
message and find the appropriate ways to communicate that in a given context. Always bear in 
mind the purpose of your communication. Don't get so involved in the communication that you 
forget the “why”. The why is so that they might be able to spread the good news or share its 
blessing. Stay focused on the goal. Paul was singular in his goal, but varied in his 
methodology. 

When we have a message to share the message is singular, but the methods by which that 
message can be communicated might be varied. And so Paul understands this and as a great 
communicator, he modifies the ‘how’ of the message while being true to the “what” and the 
“why” of the message. This is what all great communicators do. You're attentive to the context, 
creative in the methodology, and true to the goal. 

Number 45: Know When To Be Silent.  
Ecclesiastes 3:7. There's a time to be quiet and a time to speak. 

There are times when saying nothing is the best policy. Do not be driven by “I have to 
communicate”. Is it really necessary? Is it always beneficial? You know, do you really have to 
answer every tweet and every email within three minutes? Do you have to stop life? Is it that 
urgent? 

Job’s friends show true friendship when they first came to see him in his distress and they sat 
with him in silence. We read in Job 2:11-13, the following: when three of his friends heard of 
the tragedy he had suffered, they got together and traveled from their homes to comfort and 
console him. Their names were Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the 
Naamathite. When they saw Job from a distance, they scarcely recognize him. Wailing loudly. 
They tore their robs and threw dust into the air, over their heads to show their grief. Then they 
sat on the ground with him for seven days and nights. No one said a word to Job, for they saw 
that he was suffering and was too great for words. 

Sometimes there are situations where the, the suffering is too great for words. And as long as 
they were quiet, they were good friends. When they started speaking, everything started going 
south. It was no longer beneficial. Their identification with the sorrow was the best 
communication. Their silence had been the best communication. 

A number of years back, we were living in another continent. We had some dear friends who, 
the husband suffered, a major medical problem and had to be rushed in the hospital under 
intense surgery. Over the next week or two, my wife and I stayed with the wife, visited the 
hospital, helped with the children and did everything we could to help their family. During that 
time, many people sent cards and made phone calls. And at first they were like, we're so sorry. 
We're praying for you. But as time went on, they started saying, why do you think he got sick 
like this? Did he disobey God? Are you disobeying God? Are you, you know, have you let down 
your shield? And they would come and, and try to figure out why he got sick.

Friends, we don't always know why we get sick or why certain things happen to us. That's the 
story of Job. And then one day, my dear friend, the wife of, my friend who was in the hospital 
had just received a call and someone had, was blaming her for her husband's illness. And, 
trying to be helpful, I think, that person was in their own mind. But she came off the phone and 
she was crying and she was saying, came to and said, David, I just want to thank you all these 
people, trying to give them wisdom, trying to explain why my husband is sick, but you, you are 
been such a good friend. You are a friend with no wisdom.” 

June 2023Page  of 3033

Because I've not tried to solve the problem. I was just there. My wife was just there and we 
were present. We weren't trying to explain things and teach things or sort things out. That's not 
always what we're supposed to do. Being present, being silent, being caring that's enough. So 
don't always talk. Listen, be still. 

Number 46: The Right Time and Place to Speak.  
Esther 2:10 Esther had not told anyone of her nationality and family background, because 
Mordecai had directed for her not to do so. 

This is such an important lesson from this book. Because Esther listened to Mordecai and 
exercised self control over her communication, she didn't say everything she could have said 
about herself. She kept her ethnic identity a secret. It was unknown. And her appropriate 
silence enabled for this climatic moment to occur when there was the reveal of who she was in 
the presence of the king and the presence of the one who was trying to wipe out all of her 
people. And the silence at one point, and the speaking at the right time at the right place, 
turned the table on the dreaded enemy and resulted in her people being saved from a certain 
destruction. 

Listen to God, not only about what to say or how to say it, but when to say it, and he will guide 
you. Proverbs 15:23 says everyone enjoys a fitting reply. It is wonderful to say the right thing at 
the right time. 

CROSSOVER PASSAGES 
We are now ready to look at the crossover passages, how the sphere of media communication 
connects with the other spheres. And some of the examples will be examples of positive 
interaction between those two spheres. And some will show ways in which they did not work 
well together. We can learn from all of these situations. 

Number 47: Media/Communication and Family 
The crossover between Media/Communication and Family. Genesis 49:28. These are the 12 
tribes of Israel, and this is what their father said as he told his sons goodbye. He blessed each 
one with an appropriate message. 

That is a word that concludes the previous 27 verses in chapter 49 of Genesis, which Jacob, 
knowing that he's about to die called all of his sons into the room and goes one by one and 
blesses each one. He affirms each one with a unique message, their identity, speaks with their 
destiny and, and gives them a word that will guide them into the future. This is so important. 
There should be “a blessing spoken here” as the motto of every household. You know, 
sometimes you go into stores - it says German spoken here, French spoken here. “Blessing 
spoken here” should be the family motto. Home is where our communication should shape the 
identity and destiny of the next generation, giving us confidence to face the future and their 
challenges of life. Parents are crucial in establishing a culture of communication that bless the 
next generation 

Number 48: Media/Communication and Economics.  
Matthew 28:15. And this is gonna be an example where it did not go good. So the guards 
accepted the bribe and said what they were told to say. Their story spread widely among the 
Jews, and they still tell it to today. 

June 2023Page  of 3133

See, the context is this. We read the previous verses, in verses 12 to 14 that a meeting with the 
elders was called and they decided to give the soldiers a large bribe. They told the soldiers, 
you must say, Jesus' disciples came during the night while we were sleeping and they stole his 
body. If the governor hears about it, we'll stand up for you so you won't get in trouble like 
serious trouble.

Like serious trouble. The Roman guards, this select group was came from elite, military 
division. They were in groups of four. The military Roman rule was that if any one of the four 
was caught having fallen asleep during the night, all four would be killed. 

So when they say, we'll make sure you don't get into trouble- Like this is big trouble. They're 
they're gonna be dead. So if they say we fell asleep, their, their lie would make, put their life in 
peril. So they say, we're gonna pay you lots of money and we'll intervene so that you won't be 
executed. They knew they had not fallen asleep. Roman soldiers didn't do this. The body had 
not been stolen. To confess to do this would've made them guilty of the death penalty. But the 
religious and political military leaders conspired together to use finances, used the economic 
sphere, to perpetrate a lie. Not all communication is true. In this situation bribery distorted the 
truth. Economics was used to fabricate a wrong impression that was communicated publicly 
for not only at that time, but still many years later, this was the report that people believed.

Number 49: Media/Communication and the sphere of 
Government.  
Proverbs 15:22 plans go wrong for lack of advice; many advisors bring success. 

Jesus applied this principle when he gave the following example and Luke 14:31-32, he says 
this, Or what king would go toward as another king without first sitting down with his 
counselors to discuss whether his army of 10,000 could defeat the 20,000 soldiers marching 
against him. And if he can't, he will send a delegation to discuss terms of peace while the 
enemy is still far away. 

Good communication from good advisors is essential for good decision making in government 
and really in any other sphere as well. Communication plays a very key role. 

Number 50: Media/Communication and the sphere of 
Religion.  
We're reading from Ecclesiastes 5:1-7. As you enter the house of God, keep your ears open 
and your mouth shut. It is evil to make mindless offerings to God. Don't make rash promises 
and don't be hasty in bringing matters before God. After all, God is in heaven, and you are here 
on earth. So let your words be few. Too much activity gives you restless dreams; too many 
words make you a fool. When you make your promise to God, don't delay in following through, 
for God makes takes no pleasure in fools. Keep all the promises you make to him. It is better to 
say nothing than to make a promise or not keep it. 

Don't let your mouth make you sin. And don't defend yourself by telling the Temple messenger 
that the promise you made was mistake. That would make God angry, and he might wipe out 
everything you have achieved. Talk is cheap, like daydreams and other useless activities. Fear 
God instead. 

June 2023Page  of 3233

Wow, this is a good passage. Speaking about religious vows. We’re exhorted to exercise self 
control over our tongues. Keep your ears open and your mouth shut is the advice that is given. 
Sometimes less communication is the wisest communication.

title: Crossover with CELEBRATION
transcript:
Media/Communication and Celebration.  
Esther 8:17. in every province and city, wherever the king’s decree arrived, the Jews rejoiced 
and had a great celebration and declared a public festival of holiday. And many of the people 
of the land became Jews themselves, for they feared what the Jews might do to them. 

Our communication and celebration are wedded together and a continuum from the pragmatic, 
all the way to the aesthetic. All communication involves art and all art communicates. The 
function of the message and the beauty of the form are closely intertwined. It's all most 
impossible to speak of one, without speaking of the other. These two spheres are always 
working hand in hand. And if, when they both are doing it well, it result is glorious. 
June 2023Page  of 3333

title: God’s Kingdom Promotes Righteousness
category_title: God and the Sphere of Celebration
transcript:
God's Kingdom Promotes Righteousness  
Psalm 145:7 says everyone will share the story of your wonderful goodness. They will 
sing with joy about your righteousness. 

There is power in story. There's power in song. Propositional truths, just abstract 
statements about reality, about who God is, who humans are, what the universe is like, 
tend to engage only the mind, but truths that are embedded in story, truths that are 
connected with life and, and can be told in an artistic way, touch both mind and heart. 
And so they make a deeper and more lasting impression. Biblical righteousness is not 
about religious performance. It's about living in right relationships, loving relationships, 
vertically with God and horizontally with neighbor. It's about being kind and forgiving. 
It's about acting like God acts. Imitating God's character and nature, how he relates to 
people. And this like Colossians 3:12-15 describes biblical righteousness. You must 
cloth yourselves with tender hearted mercy, kindness, humility, gentleness, and 
patience. Make allowance for each other's faults and forgive anyone who offends you. 
Remember the Lord forgave you. So you must forgive others. Above all, clothe yourself 
with love, which binds us all together in perfect harmony and let the peace that comes 
from Christ rule in your hearts. For as members of one body, you are called to live at 
peace and always be thankful. 

When we use the sphere of Celebration to advance these qualities of righteousness 
and how we relate to one another, then the sphere is being used in the right way. 

June 2023Page  of 537
reflection_q1: Why is it important for celebrations to reflect God’s righteousness?
reflection_q2: How does celebrating righteousness through art, entertainment, and sport influence our community and personal actions?
call_to_action: How will this lesson shape your personal growth?

title: God’s Kingdom Promotes Peace
category_title: God and the Sphere of Celebration
transcript:
God's Kingdom Promotes Peace.  
Reading from Colossians 3: 15 and let the peace that comes from Christ rule in your 
hearts for as members of one body, you are called to live in peace. Always be thankful. 

In the world the thinking is ‘conflict sells’. So our news media is filled with stories of 
bad news. Focusing on the works of our common enemy. Wars, riots, disasters, 
economic hardship, acts of injustice. Are these tales of wrongdoing being used to 
cultivate division? Or promote unity in our society? Are we looking at the evil in the 
world and considering how do we get even? Or how do we move towards 
reconciliation and peace? 

When we think about Celebration and how we portray the stories and the events of our 
lives, we need to be people who encourage us in a movement towards peace, towards 
unity, towards harmony. This is not by being superficial or covering up, covering up 
difficulties. God does not hide problems. He's a realist, but not to live like ostriches 
with our heads' stuck in the sand, pretending if we don't see it and we don't hear it and 
we don't speak it, then it's not there. We don't live unaware of injustice or unaware of 
the things that work against peace. But we need to act in such a way and use the 
power of this sphere to advance the cause of peace and unity, relational harmony. 
That's what this sphere is all about.
reflection_q1: Why should peace be one of the ultimate goals of biblical celebration?
reflection_q2: How can peace and reconciliation be reflected through the themes and messages of arts, entertainment, and sports?
call_to_action: How can this lesson positively impact others through you?

title: God’s Kingdom Promotes Joy
category_title: God and the Sphere of Celebration
transcript:
God's Kingdom Promotes Joy.  
Zephaniah 3: 16-17. I love this passage and we often don't quote anything from the 
book of Zephaniah. So listen well. On that day, the announcement to Jerusalem will be 
cheer up. Zion. Don't be afraid for the Lord your God is living among you. He is a 
mighty savior. He will take delight in you with gladness, with his love He will calm all 
your fears. He will rejoice over you with joyful songs. 

He will rejoice over you with joyful songs. You know, when a young man falls in love 
with a young lady, you can tell almost immediately. He starts walking two feet off the 
ground, has a lilt to his step and a song in his heart. He stops talking in prose and 
begins writing poetry. Everything changes in how he communicates, in how he faces 
life. You can't help but break into poetry. You know, actually in the Bible, the first time 
poetry is used is when Adam lays his eyes on Eve for the first time. He erupts into this 
magnificent form of artistic literary communication. 

You know, to live well people need love and joy as much as they need food and water. 
And what this passage tells us that when God is in our midst, He too sings. For the 
subject of that song is you and me. He sings over us. He sings to us, He sings about 
us. Have you taken time to pause and listen to God singing to you? 

You see, joy is not a circumstantial feeling that comes and goes with changing realities. 
True joy is that deep delight which comes from the confidence that it we have when we 
look into someone's eyes and know that that someone treasures us and loves us. 
June 2023Page  of 637

When we look to God, that's what we see in His eyes. When we listen to God, that's 
what we hear in His songs. And that's what gives us joy. 

In God's presence we're told in Psalms 16: 11, there is fullness of joy. There is absolute 
joy in all his most complete expression. Why? Because He loves us fully and is always 
communicating His love to us, if we'll take time to listen, if we're willing to hear. Biblical 
celebrations are designed to help us reflect on God's joy-giving love for each one of us.
reflection_q1: How does God’s promise of joy influence our approach to celebrations?
reflection_q2: What actions can we undertake to spread this joy in our communities through art, entertainment and sports?

title: Beauty and Function
category_title: God and the Sphere of Celebration
transcript:
Beauty and Function.  
Psalm 19:1 says the heavens proclaim the glory of God, the skies display His 
craftsmanship. God's creativity is limitless. It's extraordinary. It's magnificent. It's 
breathtaking. It's both functionally excellent and aesthetically exquisite. 

When He creates with a word, He calls the galaxies to spring into existence. And 
though they function within the laws of physics, which to some might sound very dry 
and mechanistic, they also display extraordinary beauty. When we do things in this 
sphere, they should be things that are both pragmatic and aesthetic. They function, it 
works. It's real. And, and it's also beautiful and delightful, winsome and attractive.
reflection_q1: Why is understanding beauty and functionality important in reflecting God's limitless creativity?
reflection_q2: In what ways can the concept of beauty and functionality inspire artists and creators in their work to reflect God's craftsmanship?

title: Masterfully Created to Be Master Creators
category_title: God and the Sphere of Celebration
transcript:
Masterfully Created to Be Master Creators.  
Ephesians 2: 10 says for, we are God's masterpiece. He has created us anew in Christ 
Jesus. So we can do good things He planned for us long ago. 

The word in Greek is we are God's poeme where we get the English word poem. We 
can write in many different ways, but poetry is more elegant. It's more artistic than just 
prose. It requires additional thought. You think about rhyme and meter and, and 
symbolism. And you do a lot of things through indirect allusions. It's a masterful way to 
write. Many people can write prose well. Only a few can write poetry well. God says 
that we are his poems. We are his masterpiece artistic expression, And he's made us 
to participate with him in being creators. He's made us to do good things in every 
sphere, including the sphere of Celebration. Not all celebration that is done in the world 
is good. We’re to do... We're called to do good celebration. What are the good things 
that enable arts, entertainment and sports to make a beneficial contribution to our 
society? We know that these very powerful tools have been used to destroy lives of 
individuals and families of corrupt society, but they when used correctly, they're good 
things that I can participate in in this sphere that can advance God's masterpiece plan.
reflection_q1: Why is understanding our identity as God’s masterpiece crucial for how artists, entertainers, and athletes approach their vocations?
reflection_q2: How can individuals in the celebration sphere utilize their God-given creativity and skills to make a positive contribution to society?

title: Beauty Within and Without
category_title: God and the Sphere of Celebration
transcript:
Beauty Within and Without.  
We read from Isaiah 53:2 the following, my servant grew up in the Lord's presence like 
a tender a green shoot, like a root in dry ground. There was nothing beautiful and 
majestic about his appearance. Nothing to attract us to him 

In this passage, it's a prophecy of the coming Messiah, of Jesus. And what it is telling 
us is that God steps into our world in such a way that there wasn't a big splash, a big 
June 2023Page  of 737

there wasn't a red carpet and a trumpet sounding and saying, look, what a magnificent 
thing is happening. 

He was born in a very ordinary way. He lived as a, in a very ordinary way. He was 
common to us. Incarnation is absolutely extraordinary. The glorious majestic wondrous 
king of the universe stepped into our ordinary world and just was with us. He was like 
us, Hebrews says, in every way, except that he did not sin. And he brings life-giving 
beauty into the ordinary things of life. 30 years he lived in our midst. It was kind of 
unsuspecting. No one would've thought. And the neighbors grew up with and said, ah, 
look at this guy. He's the son of God. He's God incarnate. He was just one of us. There 
was nothing majestic about his appearance. He was just like all the others. 

But when he began his ministry in our midst, wow, the difference it made. 2 Corinthians 
4:7 says something similar about what happens with God's spirit inhabits our hearts. 
He said that we are like fragile clay jars containing this great treasure. God was in 
Christ. God and man blended together in just a remarkable indescribable way. This 
treasure and in a clay jar. And that true beauty that is within him, changes us. We're 
attracted to Jesus, not because of, of externals, but because of his heart, his love, his 
sacrifice, his identification with us. Jesus makes all the difference. It's not a beauty of 
externals, but a transforming eternal beauty that calls us all to Himself. 

SECTION INTRODUCTION 
In this next section, we're gonna be looking at 14 passages that focus on the purpose 
of celebration. Celebration is very purposeful and very powerful. It is the least 
authoritative and the most, perhaps the most influential of all the spheres. If we 
understand why God gave us this sphere, we can use it in a way that really enhances 
God's purposes on earth. So let's consider these things together.
reflection_q1: Why is it important for society to recognize and value inner beauty and character?
reflection_q2: In what ways can the celebration sphere challenge and promote a deeper understanding and appreciation of true beauty?

title: Affirms That We Are Destined for Joy
transcript:
Celebration Affirms that We are Destined 
for Joy.  
Hebrews 12: 1- 2 says this. Therefore, since we are surrounded by such a huge crowd 
of witnesses to the life of faith, let us strip off every weight that slows us down, 
especially this sin, that so easily trips us up and let us run with endurance the race that 
God has set before us. We do this by keeping our eyes on Jesus, the champion who 
initiates and perfects our faith because of the joy awaiting him. He endured the cross, 
disregarding its shame. Now he is seated in the place of honor beside God's throne. 

This phrase that that is so interesting is “because of the joy awaiting him”. Jesus was 
able to embrace a lot of very difficult, painful things. The cross was costly. But he had a 
destiny in which he was aware of joy. And of course, as we looked at it earlier passage, 
he's thinking about the restored relationship with the Father. Psalms 16: 11 says that in 
God's presence, there was fullness of joy and he was expecting to be returned to the 
Father with all of that relational richness. And that motivated him. This needs to be 
what motivates us in the sphere of Celebration. We see this theme throughout the 
Bible. In chapter 15 of Luke, there are several parables that are only found in that 
June 2023Page  of 837

portion of the Bible. There's a lost coin, the lost sheep and the lost son. We often call 
the prodigal son and each of these stories, the idea is that something or someone gets 
lost and then they get found and get recovered and they are. And when that happens, 
there's a concluding statement. This is a symbol of what happens when a sinner 
repents and is restored to relationship with God. And in verse seven and verse 10 in 
chapter 15 of Luke, it says that when the, this happens there's joy before the angels of 
heaven when a sinner repents. There is joy before the angels of heaven, that's an 
interesting phrase. Think about it. If you are an angel in heaven, what would you be 
looking at? Now, you wouldn't be looking at streets of gold or this or that or the other. 
You've been there all your time, and those are just like common, ordinary things. If I 
was an angel in heaven, I'd be looking at the one who is the centerpiece attraction. 
You're looking to the person of God all the time. So when it says when they rejoice, 
because they're, they saw joy before the angels is that they're seeing God face erupt in 
joy when a sinner repents. It's not a casual, oh, yes. One more for the kingdom. Lets 
notch it up. It's not a task that God is dutifully fulfilling. He delights. When a person 
comes to faith in Jesus, he throws a party. He calls his friends. He celebrates. It's real.

You are welcome. And I want you to be safe. This was a very, very high value in the 
cultures of the ancient middle east. The value of hospitality was supreme. If you

title: Builds Community
transcript:

Numbers 21:8- 9. Then the Lord told him that's Moses, who was facing a difficult

title: Use and Abuse of the Arts
transcript:

We should not absolutize pleasure, which is called hedonism, making that the ultimate 
goal. It's destructive to the individual and to society. If pleasure is the goal, we are on a 
dangerous ground, but when pleasure is the fruit of our pursuit of a loving God and His 
good and kind and righteous purposes in our lives, then we are on solid ground. Let's 
keep pleasure as the fruit and keep God as the goal. 

SECTION INTRODUCTION 
This next section is about the five senses in God's design. As we're gonna look at 11

sphere of celebration. This is really the overarching conclusion of the purpose. This 
could have been the culminating passage for the last section on the purpose of 
celebration, but it's also the introduction to this section about how we engage with the

apostles did, as John writes, and it is then shared, it is done in such a way that others

title: Taste: An Enjoyable Gift

transcript:
Taste: An Enjoyable Gift.  
Ecclessiates 2:24-26. So I decided there was nothing better than to enjoy food and

We've all enjoyed a good meal. Taste is absolutely an incredible gift from God. We

You know, as I travel the world and visit our missionary friends in different places, I

special. 

You know, there are special flavors that you associate with the good memories of your

We all eat food. We all use spices, but the way they're combined gives us uniqueness. 
And so taste shapes our unique cultural communities; that gives us a sense of

title: Taste: Culinary Excellence Shows Honor
category_title: The Five Senses and God’s Design
transcript:
Taste: (Culinary Excellence Shows Honor) 
Once again, we look at taste a second time. Culinary excellence shows honor. 1

Wow. In every culture, there's a special food that you bring out when there are special

have to work at that smile, because people are trying to give you their best. 

Food, taste, honor, relationship. They're all connected. Think about this. We build 
relationships around food.  Leonard Sweet has written a great book From Tablet to

June 2023Page  of 1937

your neighbors, share a meal, invite someone who's homeless. Someone who you 
don't know so well. Show hospitality. Allow taste to be a bridge to declare “I care for

Number 24, Touch. (Strengthens Relationship)  
This is the second of the five senses the move. We'll be looking at two passages on 
touch. This one is how touch Strengthens Relationship. It comes from 1 Samuel 18:4, 
Jonathan sealed a pact that he made by with David by taking off his robe and giving it 
to David together with his tunic sword, bow and belt. 

I wonder what that looked like. There's not a lot of details in scripture, but I'm sure he 
just didn't throw these down at David feet and Say, take them. They're yours. He 
probably handed it to David touched him. They exchanged something that was an 
intimate gift giving time. 

I sometimes ponder on Genesis 3 after Adam and Eve sin. This says that God to, um, 
models, what it was necessary to restore them from sin and sacrifices an animal and, 
and takes the skins and prepares clothes. This must have taken some time. They were 
watching him. You don't just kill an animal and, and in an instant have clothes. And this

Touch is important. We can touch in right ways and we can touch in wrong ways. Our 
different cultures have different ideas about touch and what's appropriate. And what's 
inappropriate. As I've traveled into over 200 nations of the earth, I've learned a lot of

when you'll get introduced to someone new, you will extend your hand and, and

oh, he knows my culture 

And in North America, you tend to shake hands and very close; in Europe, you shake 
hands a little further away. And in Asia touches too, too intimate. If you just meet

quite they're all right within their culture. They're all exhibiting different forms of values

June 2023Page  of 2037

what is considered appropriate - tells you something about the community you're from 
that establishes your identity, your cultural values. We need to touch in ways that are
